ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for commercial wafer bar product; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of Loacker wafer bar is one bar weighing approximately 25 g, providing around 131 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Loacker Wafer Bar — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Loacker wafer bar (any flavour), CR ID F11080031. Sugar reported as 0.0 g/100 g and dietary fibre as 0.0 g/100 g in the HPB record; these values are preserved as reported but flagged as atypical in notes. Saturated fat estimated at approximately 14.5 g/100 g based on typical palm-oil-based wafer bar composition cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central entry for similar wafer products (USDA FDC ID 168936, Wafers, vanilla, cream-filled).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Loacker-brand wafer bars in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 76 is estimated from the closest analogous food: cream-filled wafer biscuits (wheat flour base, cream filling, high refined carbohydrate, negligible fibre), consistent with GI values reported for similar wafer and cream-biscuit products in Atkinson et al. (2008) and the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (76 × 56.75 × 0.25) / 100 = 10.8 g per 25 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High saturated fat and refined carbohydrate load with zero dietary fibre
hypertension.guidance: Sodium is moderate at 127 mg/100 g; however, the estimated saturated fat of ~14.5 g/100 g and high refined carbohydrate load (56.75 g/100 g with 0 g fibre) contribute to arterial stiffness and insulin-driven sodium retention — individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and frequency of consumption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate (56.75 g/100 g) with zero dietary fibre and high estimated GI (~76)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The combination of 56.75 g/100 g refined carbohydrate, 0 g dietary fibre, and an estimated GI of 76 produces a high glycaemic load; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this product as it will cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High estimated saturated fat (~14.5 g/100 g) from palm-based vegetable fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The estimated saturated fat content of approximately 14.5 g/100 g, likely derived from palm oil, significantly exceeds the recommended limit for cardiovascular health; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of this product.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~14.5 g/100 g from palm-based vegetable fat exceeds the 5 g/100 g RA threshold
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Palm-based saturated fat promotes pro-inflammatory cytokines (TNF-alpha, IL-6); individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this product and check the ingredient list for palm oil or partially hydrogenated vegetable fat.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Loacker Wafer Bar is a commercially produced, ultra-processed crispy layered wafer biscuit with cream filling, manufactured by Loacker, an Italian confectionery brand originating from South Tyrol. The product is available in multiple flavours (e.g., vanilla, chocolate, hazelnut) and is widely distributed in Singapore and across Asia. The wafer layers are thin, light, and crunchy, sandwiching a smooth, sweet cream filling. Per 100 g, the product provides 523 kcal, 7.8 g protein, 29.14 g fat (estimated ~14.5 g saturated fat from palm-based vegetable fat), and 56.75 g carbohydrate with 0 g dietary fibre as reported by the HPB Singapore database. The sugar value of 0 g/100 g reported in the HPB source is atypical for a cream-filled wafer bar and should be verified against the product packaging label, as actual sugar content is likely higher. The product has a high estimated glycaemic index (~76) and a high glycaemic load per serving (~10.8 per 25 g bar), making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. The high saturated fat content raises cardiovascular and inflammatory concerns. This product is classified as ultra-processed and is best treated as an occasional treat rather than a regular dietary item.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Loacker wafer bar?
Can diabetics eat Loacker wafer bars?
Is Loacker wafer bar high in sugar?
What is the fat content of Loacker wafer?
Is Loacker wafer bar suitable for hypertension?
Loacker wafer bar nutrition facts Singapore
Loacker cream wafer calories per bar

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sodium) sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID F11080031) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated at ~14.5 g/100 g using USDA FoodData Central analogous wafer product as cross-reference. Sugar (0 g) and dietary fibre (0 g) preserved as reported by HPB but flagged as potentially atypical. GI estimated from analogous cream-filled wafer biscuit data in the University of Sydney GI Database.
notes: 1. Sugar_g is reported as 0.0 g/100 g by the HPB Singapore Foodo database. This value is atypical for a cream-filled flavoured wafer bar and should be verified against the current product packaging label; actual sugar content is likely higher. The 0.0 value is preserved as reported (confirmed zero per HPB source) pending label verification. 2. Dietary_fibre_g is reported as 0.0 g/100 g by HPB; this is plausible for a refined-flour wafer product with no whole-grain ingredients (confirmed zero per HPB source). 3. Calcium_mg is reported as 0.0 mg/100 g and iron_mg as 0.0 mg/100 g by HPB; these are preserved as reported (confirmed zero per HPB source) though trace amounts may be present below detection threshold. 4. Saturated_fat_g is not directly reported in the HPB source; the value of 14.5 g/100 g is estimated from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 168936 (Wafers, vanilla, cream-filled) as the closest analogous approved-source entry. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, and all vitamin and mineral fields not reported by HPB are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, cholesterol, potassium, and micronutrient data for this specific product are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 6. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the HPB source for this product. 7. GI of 76 is an analogous estimate based on cream-filled wafer biscuit data from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. (2008); no direct Loacker-brand GI measurement was found in approved sources.

Added glycemic_index (76, analogous estimate), gi_category ('high'), glycemic_load_per_serving (10.8), and gi_source from University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. (2008).
Added health_context for Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and Cardiovascular Disease with specific clinical guidance; retained Rheumatoid Arthritis as additional condition.

Documented zero-value fields (sugar_g, dietary_fibre_g, calcium_mg, iron_mg) with 'confirmed zero per HPB source' justification in notes.

Added llm_training section with natural_language_description exceeding 200 characters, common_user_queries, meal_context, and ingredient_synonyms.
Set confidence to 'high' with documented reason.
